contact_info.brief_description_of_your_business_and_o:
Cedar Creek Berry & Pumpkin Farm is a family-run agritourism farm located just outside Blythewood in northern Richland County. We specialize in pick-your-own seasonal fruits and family-friendly farm experiences. From late April through June, visitors can pick strawberries and early blueberries in our irrigated, mulched fields, with shaded rest areas and hand-washing stations located throughout the property. In the summer, we offer limited u-pick blackberries and a small cut-flower garden, along with a farm stand featuring our own sweet corn, tomatoes, cucumbers, squash, and pasture-raised eggs.

Our busiest season is fall, when we open our pumpkin patch, 5-acre corn maze, hayrides along Cedar Creek, and a small barnyard area with goats, sheep, and laying hens. We host school field trips during the week with age-appropriate educational stations that cover topics like soil health, pollinators, and how fruits and vegetables are grown in South Carolina. On weekends, we offer family activities such as a kids’ straw-bale maze, barrel-train rides, and campfire s’mores in the evenings by reservation.

The farm store carries seasonal produce grown on-site or by neighboring farms, along with jams, fruit syrups, pickles, local honey, and simple baked goods like strawberry bread and pumpkin muffins made in a certified kitchen. We place a strong emphasis on sustainable practices, using drip irrigation, cover crops, and integrated pest management to reduce chemical inputs while keeping fields visitor-friendly. Our goal is to give families, school groups, and visitors a chance to slow down, get their hands in the dirt, and learn where their food comes from in a relaxed, authentic farm setting. Group tours, birthday parties, photography sessions, and small private events can be scheduled in advance, and updated seasonal hours and crop availability are always posted on our website and social media pages.
